Commands.Name: html5-list
Commands.HelpText: Display list of HTML5 applications or file paths of specified application

UsageDetails.Usage: cf html5-list [APP_NAME] [APP_VERSION] [APP_HOST_ID|-n APP_HOST_NAME] [-d|-di DESTINATION_SERVICE_INSTANCE_NAME|-a CF_APP_NAME [-rt RUNTIME] [-u]]

Options.-app, -a: Cloud Foundry application name, which is bound to services that expose UI via html5-apps-repo
Options.-destination, -d: List HTML5 applications exposed via subaccount destinations with sap.cloud.service and html5-apps-repo.app_host_id properties
Options.-destination-instance, -di: List HTML5 applications exposed via service instance destinations with sap.cloud.service and html5-apps-repo.app_host_id properties
Options.-name, -n: Use html5-apps-repo app-host service instance name instead of APP_HOST_ID
Options.-runtime, -rt: Runtime service for which conventional URLs of applications will be shown. Default value is 'cpp'
Options.-url, -u: Show conventional URLs of applications, when accessed via Cloud Foundry application specified with --app flag or when --destination or --destination-instance flag is used
Options.APP_HOST_ID: GUID of html5-apps-repo app-host service instance that contains application with specified name and version
Options.APP_HOST_NAME: Name of html5-apps-repo app-host service instance that contains application with specified name and version
Options.APP_NAME: Application name, which file paths should be listed. If not provided, list of applications will be printed
Options.APP_VERSION: Application version, which file paths should be listed. If not provided, current active version will be used

Commands.Name: html5-get
Commands.HelpText: Fetch content of single HTML5 application file by path, or whole application by name and version

Options.-all: Flag that indicates that all applications of specified APP_HOST_ID or APP_HOST_NAME should be fetched
Options.-name, -n: Use html5-apps-repo app-host service instance name instead of APP_HOST_ID
Options.-out, -o: Output file (for single file) or output directory (for application). By default, standard output and current working directory
Options.APP_HOST_ID: GUID of html5-apps-repo app-host service instance that contains application with specified name and version
Options.APP_HOST_NAME: Name of html5-apps-repo app-host service instance that contains application with specified name and version

Commands.Name: html5-push
Commands.HelpText: Push HTML5 applications to html5-apps-repo service

UsageDetails.Usage: cf html5-push [-d|-di DESTINATION_SERVICE_INSTANCE_NAME|-s SERVICE_INSTANCE_NAME] [-rt RUNTIME] [-r|-n APP_HOST_NAME] [PATH_TO_APP_FOLDER ...] [APP_HOST_ID]

Options.-destination,-d: Create subaccount level destination with credentials to access HTML5 applications
Options.-destination-instance, -di: Create service instance level destination with credentials to access HTML5 applications
Options.-name,-n: Use app-host service instance with specified name
Options.-redeploy,-r: Redeploy HTML5 applications. All applications should be previously deployed to same service instance
Options.-runtime, -rt: Runtime service for which conventional URLs of applications will be shown. Default value is 'cpp'
Options.-service,-s: Create subaccount level destination with credentials of the service instance
Options.APP_HOST_ID: GUID of html5-apps-repo app-host service instance that contains application with specified name and version
Options.APP_HOST_NAME: Name of app-host service instance to which applications should be deployed
Options.PATH_TO_APP_FOLDER: One or multiple paths to folders containing manifest.json and xs-app.json files

Commands.Name: html5-delete
Commands.HelpText: Delete one or multiple app-host service instances or content uploaded with these instances

Options.-destination,-d: delete subaccount level destinations that point to service instances to be deleted
Options.-destination-instance, -di: delete destinations that point to service instances to be deleted from specific destination service instance
Options.-name,-n: Use app-host service instance with specified name
Options.APP_HOST_ID: GUID of html5-apps-repo app-host service instance
Options.APP_HOST_NAME: Name of html5-apps-repo app-host service instance

Commands.Name: html5-info
Commands.HelpText: Get size limit and status of app-host service instances

Options.-name,-n: Use app-host service instance with specified name
Options.APP_HOST_ID: GUID of html5-apps-repo app-host service instance
Options.APP_HOST_NAME: Name of html5-apps-repo app-host service instance

Commands.Name: deploy
Commands.HelpText: Deploy a new multi-target app or sync changes to an existing one

UsageDetails.Usage:
Deploy a multi-target app archive

   cf deploy MTA [-e EXT_DESCRIPTOR[,...]] [-t TIMEOUT] [--version-rule VERSION_RULE] [-u URL] [-f] [--retries RETRIES] [--no-start] [--namespace NAMESPACE] [--apply-namespace-app-names true/false] [--apply-namespace-service-names true/false] [--apply-namespace-app-routes true/false] [--apply-namespace-as-suffix true/false ] [--delete-services] [--delete-service-keys] [--delete-service-brokers] [--keep-files] [--no-restart-subscribed-apps] [--do-not-fail-on-missing-permissions] [--abort-on-error] [--strategy STRATEGY] [--skip-testing-phase] [--skip-idle-start] [--require-secure-parameters] [--disposable-user-provided-service] [--apps-start-timeout TIMEOUT] [--apps-stage-timeout TIMEOUT] [--apps-upload-timeout TIMEOUT] [--apps-task-execution-timeout TIMEOUT]

   Perform action on an active deploy operation
   cf deploy -i OPERATION_ID -a ACTION [-u URL]

   Deploy a multi-target app archive referenced by a remote URL
   <write MTA archive URL to STDOUT> | cf deploy [-e EXT_DESCRIPTOR[,...]] [-t TIMEOUT] [--version-rule VERSION_RULE] [-u MTA_CONTROLLER_URL] [--retries RETRIES] [--no-start] [--namespace NAMESPACE] [--apply-namespace-app-names true/false] [--apply-namespace-service-names true/false] [--apply-namespace-app-routes true/false] [--apply-namespace-as-suffix true/false ] [--delete-services] [--delete-service-keys] [--delete-service-brokers] [--keep-files] [--no-restart-subscribed-apps] [--do-not-fail-on-missing-permissions] [--abort-on-error] [--strategy STRATEGY] [--skip-testing-phase] [--skip-idle-start] [require-secure-parameters] [--disposable-user-provided-service] [--apps-start-timeout TIMEOUT] [--apps-stage-timeout TIMEOUT] [--apps-upload-timeout TIMEOUT] [--apps-task-execution-timeout TIMEOUT]

ENVIRONMENT:
   DEBUG=1                                         Enables the logging of HTTP requests in STDOUT and STDERR.
   MULTIAPPS_CONTROLLER_URL=<URL>                  Overrides the default deploy-service.<system-domain> with a custom URL.
   MULTIAPPS_USER_AGENT_SUFFIX=<STRING>            Appends custom text to User-Agent header. Only alphanumeric, spaces, hyphens, dots, underscores allowed. Max 128 chars, excess truncated.

   MULTIAPPS_UPLOAD_CHUNK_SIZE=<POSITIVE_INTEGER>  Configures chunk size (in MB) for MTAR upload.
   MULTIAPPS_UPLOAD_CHUNKS_SEQUENTIALLY=<BOOLEAN>  Upload chunks sequentially instead of in parallel. By default is false.
   MULTIAPPS_DISABLE_UPLOAD_PROGRESS_BAR=<BOOLEAN> Disable upload progress bar (useful in CI/CD). By default is false.

Options.-abort-on-error: Auto-abort the process on any errors
Options.-all-modules: Deploy all modules which are contained in the deployment descriptor, in the current location
Options.-all-resources: Deploy all resources which are contained in the deployment descriptor, in the current location
Options.-apply-namespace-app-names: Apply namespace to application names: (true, false)
Options.-apply-namespace-app-routes: Apply namespace to application routes: (true, false)
Options.-apply-namespace-as-suffix: Apply namespace as a suffix rather than a prefix: (true, false)
Options.-apply-namespace-service-names: Apply namespace to service names: (true, false)
Options.-backup-previous-version: (EXPERIMENTAL) (STRATEGY: BLUE-GREEN, INCREMENTAL-BLUE-GREEN) Backup previous version of applications, use new cli command "rollback-mta" to rollback to the previous version
Options.-delete-service-keys: Delete existing service keys and apply the new ones
Options.-delete-services: Recreate changed services / delete discontinued services
Options.-dependency-aware-stop-order: (EXPERIMENTAL) (STRATEGY: BLUE-GREEN, INCREMENTAL-BLUE-GREEN) Stop apps in a dependency-aware order during the resume phase of a blue-green deployment
Options.-disposable-user-provided-service: Deploy when --require-secure-parameters flag is active for disposable UPS to be created and then deleted at the of the operation
Options.-do-not-fail-on-missing-permissions: Do not fail on missing permissions for admin operations
Options.-namespace: Namespace for the MTA, applied on app names, app routes and service names
Options.-no-restart-subscribed-apps: Do not restart subscribed apps, updated during the deployment
Options.-require-secure-parameters: (EXPERIMENTAL) Pass secrets to the deploy service in a secure way
Options.-retries: Retry the operation N times in case a non-content error occurs (default 3)
Options.-skip-idle-start: (STRATEGY: BLUE-GREEN, INCREMENTAL-BLUE-GREEN) Directly start the new MTA version as 'live', skipping the 'idle' phase of the resources. Do not require further confirmation or testing before deleting the old version
Options.-skip-testing-phase: (STRATEGY: BLUE-GREEN, INCREMENTAL-BLUE-GREEN) Do not require confirmation for deleting the previously deployed MTA app
Options.-strategy: Specify the deployment strategy when updating an mta (default, blue-green, incremental-blue-green)
Options.a: Action to perform on active deploy operation (abort, retry, resume, monitor)
Options.f: Force deploy without confirmation for aborting conflicting processes
Options.m: Deploy list of modules which are contained in the deployment descriptor, in the current location
Options.r: Deploy list of resources which are contained in the deployment descriptor, in the current location

Commands.Name: bg-deploy
Commands.HelpText: Deploy a multi-target app using blue-green deployment

UsageDetails.Usage:
Deploy a multi-target app using blue-green deployment
   cf bg-deploy MTA [-e EXT_DESCRIPTOR[,...]] [-t TIMEOUT] [--version-rule VERSION_RULE] [-u URL] [-f] [--retries RETRIES] [--no-start] [--namespace NAMESPACE] [--apply-namespace-app-names true/false] [--apply-namespace-service-names true/false] [--apply-namespace-app-routes true/false] [--apply-namespace-as-suffix true/false ] [--delete-services] [--delete-service-keys] [--delete-service-brokers] [--keep-files] [--no-restart-subscribed-apps] [--no-confirm] [--skip-idle-start] [--do-not-fail-on-missing-permissions] [--abort-on-error] [--apps-start-timeout TIMEOUT] [--apps-stage-timeout TIMEOUT] [--apps-upload-timeout TIMEOUT] [--apps-task-execution-timeout TIMEOUT]

   Perform action on an active deploy operation
   cf deploy -i OPERATION_ID -a ACTION [-u URL] 

ENVIRONMENT:
   DEBUG=1                                         Enables the logging of HTTP requests in STDOUT and STDERR.
   MULTIAPPS_CONTROLLER_URL=<URL>                  Overrides the default deploy-service.<system-domain> with a custom URL.
   MULTIAPPS_USER_AGENT_SUFFIX=<STRING>            Appends custom text to User-Agent header. Only alphanumeric, spaces, hyphens, dots, underscores allowed. Max 128 chars, excess truncated.

   MULTIAPPS_UPLOAD_CHUNK_SIZE=<POSITIVE_INTEGER>  Configures chunk size (in MB) for MTAR upload.
   MULTIAPPS_UPLOAD_CHUNKS_SEQUENTIALLY=<BOOLEAN>  Upload chunks sequentially instead of in parallel. By default is false.
   MULTIAPPS_DISABLE_UPLOAD_PROGRESS_BAR=<BOOLEAN> Disable upload progress bar (useful in CI/CD). By default is false.

Options.-abort-on-error: Auto-abort the process on any errors
Options.-apply-namespace-app-names: Apply namespace to application names: (true, false)
Options.-apply-namespace-app-routes: Apply namespace to application routes: (true, false)
Options.-apply-namespace-as-suffix: Apply namespace as a suffix rather than a prefix: (true, false)
Options.-apply-namespace-service-names: Apply namespace to service names: (true, false)
Options.-backup-previous-version: (EXPERIMENTAL) Backup previous version of applications, use new cli command "rollback-mta" to rollback to the previous version
Options.-delete-service-keys: Delete existing service keys and apply the new ones
Options.-delete-services: Recreate changed services / delete discontinued services
Options.-do-not-fail-on-missing-permissions: Do not fail on missing permissions for admin operations
Options.-namespace: Namespace for the MTA, applied on app names, app routes and service names
Options.-no-confirm: Do not require confirmation for deleting the previously deployed MTA apps
Options.-no-restart-subscribed-apps: Do not restart subscribed apps, updated during the deployment
Options.-retries: Retry the operation N times in case a non-content error occurs (default 3)
Options.-skip-idle-start: Directly start the new MTA version as 'live', skipping the 'idle' phase of the resources. Do not require further confirmation or testing before deleting the old version
Options.a: Action to perform on active deploy operation (abort, retry, resume, monitor)
Options.f: Force deploy without confirmation for aborting conflicting processes

UsageDetails.Usage:
cf mtas [-u URL]

ENVIRONMENT:
   DEBUG=1                                         Enables the logging of HTTP requests in STDOUT and STDERR.
   MULTIAPPS_CONTROLLER_URL=<URL>                  Overrides the default deploy-service.<system-domain> with a custom URL.
   MULTIAPPS_USER_AGENT_SUFFIX=<STRING>            Appends custom text to User-Agent header. Only alphanumeric, spaces, hyphens, dots, underscores allowed. Max 128 chars, excess truncated.

Commands.Name: download-mta-op-logs
Commands.HelpText: Download logs of multi-target app operation

UsageDetails.Usage:
cf download-mta-op-logs -i OPERATION_ID [-d DIRECTORY] [-u URL]

   cf download-mta-op-logs --mta MTA [--last NUM] [-d DIRECTORY] [-u URL]

ENVIRONMENT:
   DEBUG=1                                         Enables the logging of HTTP requests in STDOUT and STDERR.
   MULTIAPPS_CONTROLLER_URL=<URL>                  Overrides the default deploy-service.<system-domain> with a custom URL.
   MULTIAPPS_USER_AGENT_SUFFIX=<STRING>            Appends custom text to User-Agent header. Only alphanumeric, spaces, hyphens, dots, underscores allowed. Max 128 chars, excess truncated.

Options.-last: Downloads last NUM operation logs. If not specified, logs for each process with the specified MTA_ID are downloaded
Options.d: Root directory to download logs, by default the current working directory

UsageDetails.Usage:
Undeploy a multi-target app
   cf undeploy MTA_ID [-u URL] [-f] [--retries RETRIES] [--namespace NAMESPACE] [--delete-services] [--delete-service-keys] [--delete-service-brokers] [--no-restart-subscribed-apps] [--do-not-fail-on-missing-permissions] [--abort-on-error]

   Perform action on an active undeploy operation
   cf undeploy -i OPERATION_ID -a ACTION [-u URL]

ENVIRONMENT:
   DEBUG=1                                         Enables the logging of HTTP requests in STDOUT and STDERR.
   MULTIAPPS_CONTROLLER_URL=<URL>                  Overrides the default deploy-service.<system-domain> with a custom URL.
   MULTIAPPS_USER_AGENT_SUFFIX=<STRING>            Appends custom text to User-Agent header. Only alphanumeric, spaces, hyphens, dots, underscores allowed. Max 128 chars, excess truncated.

Options.-abort-on-error: Auto-abort the process on any errors
Options.-do-not-fail-on-missing-permissions: Do not fail on missing permissions for admin operations
Options.-namespace: Specify the (optional) namespace the target mta is in
Options.-no-restart-subscribed-apps: Do not restart subscribed apps, updated during the undeployment
Options.-retries: Retry the operation N times in case a non-content error occurs (default 3)
Options.a: Action to perform on the active undeploy operation (abort, retry, monitor)

Commands.Name: mta
Commands.HelpText: Display health and status for a multi-target app

UsageDetails.Usage:
cf mta MTA_ID [--namespace NAMESPACE] [-u URL]

ENVIRONMENT:
   DEBUG=1                                         Enables the logging of HTTP requests in STDOUT and STDERR.
   MULTIAPPS_CONTROLLER_URL=<URL>                  Overrides the default deploy-service.<system-domain> with a custom URL.
   MULTIAPPS_USER_AGENT_SUFFIX=<STRING>            Appends custom text to User-Agent header. Only alphanumeric, spaces, hyphens, dots, underscores allowed. Max 128 chars, excess truncated.

Options.-namespace: namespace of the requested mta, empty by default

UsageDetails.Usage:
cf mta-ops [--mta MTA] [-u URL] [--last NUM] [--all]

ENVIRONMENT:
   DEBUG=1                                         Enables the logging of HTTP requests in STDOUT and STDERR.
   MULTIAPPS_CONTROLLER_URL=<URL>                  Overrides the default deploy-service.<system-domain> with a custom URL.
   MULTIAPPS_USER_AGENT_SUFFIX=<STRING>            Appends custom text to User-Agent header. Only alphanumeric, spaces, hyphens, dots, underscores allowed. Max 128 chars, excess truncated.

Options.-all: List all operations, not just the active ones

Commands.Name: purge-mta-config
Commands.HelpText: Purge no longer valid configuration entries

UsageDetails.Usage:
cf purge-mta-config [-u URL]

ENVIRONMENT:
   DEBUG=1                                         Enables the logging of HTTP requests in STDOUT and STDERR.
   MULTIAPPS_CONTROLLER_URL=<URL>                  Overrides the default deploy-service.<system-domain> with a custom URL.
   MULTIAPPS_USER_AGENT_SUFFIX=<STRING>            Appends custom text to User-Agent header. Only alphanumeric, spaces, hyphens, dots, underscores allowed. Max 128 chars, excess truncated.

Commands.Name: rollback-mta
Commands.HelpText: (EXPERIMENTAL) Rollback of a multi-target app works only if [--backup-previous-version] flag was used during blue-green deployment and backup applications exists in the space

UsageDetails.Usage:
Rollback of a multi-target app
   cf rollback-mta MTA_ID [-t TIMEOUT] [-f] [--retries RETRIES] [--namespace NAMESPACE] [--do-not-fail-on-missing-permissions] [--abort-on-error] [--apps-start-timeout TIMEOUT] [--apps-stage-timeout TIMEOUT] [--apps-upload-timeout TIMEOUT] [--apps-task-execution-timeout TIMEOUT]

   Perform action on an active deploy operation
   cf rollback-mta -i OPERATION_ID -a ACTION [-u URL]

ENVIRONMENT:
   DEBUG=1                                         Enables the logging of HTTP requests in STDOUT and STDERR.
   MULTIAPPS_CONTROLLER_URL=<URL>                  Overrides the default deploy-service.<system-domain> with a custom URL.
   MULTIAPPS_USER_AGENT_SUFFIX=<STRING>            Appends custom text to User-Agent header. Only alphanumeric, spaces, hyphens, dots, underscores allowed. Max 128 chars, excess truncated.

Options.-abort-on-error: Auto-abort the process on any errors
Options.-delete-services: Recreate changed services / delete discontinued services
Options.-do-not-fail-on-missing-permissions: Do not fail on missing permissions for admin operations
Options.-namespace: Namespace for the MTA, applied on app names, app routes and service names
Options.-process-user-provided-services: Enable processing of user provided services during rollback
Options.-retries: Retry the operation N times in case a non-content error occurs (default 3)
Options.a: Action to perform on active deploy operation (abort, retry, resume, monitor)
Options.f: Force deploy without confirmation for aborting conflicting processes
